     1	import { existsSync, readdirSync, readFileSync } from "fs"
     2	import { basename, join } from "path"
     3	import {
     4	  parseFrontmatter,
     5	  sanitizeModelField,
     6	  findProjectOpencodeCommandDirs,
     7	  getOpenCodeCommandDirs,
     8	  discoverPluginCommandDefinitions,
     9	} from "../../shared"
    10	import type { CommandFrontmatter } from "../../features/claude-code-command-loader/types"
    11	import { isMarkdownFile } from "../../shared/file-utils"
    12	import { getClaudeConfigDir } from "../../shared"
    13	import { loadBuiltinCommands } from "../../features/builtin-commands"
    14	import type { CommandInfo, CommandMetadata, CommandScope } from "./types"
    15	
    16	export interface CommandDiscoveryOptions {
    17	  pluginsEnabled?: boolean
    18	  enabledPluginsOverride?: Record<string, boolean>
    19	}
    20	
    21	const NESTED_COMMAND_SEPARATOR = "/"
    22	
    23	function discoverCommandsFromDir(
    24	  commandsDir: string,
    25	  scope: CommandScope,
    26	  prefix = "",
    27	): CommandInfo[] {
    28	  if (!existsSync(commandsDir)) return []
    29	
    30	  const entries = readdirSync(commandsDir, { withFileTypes: true })
    31	  const commands: CommandInfo[] = []
    32	
    33	  for (const entry of entries) {
    34	    if (entry.isDirectory()) {
    35	      if (entry.name.startsWith(".")) continue
    36	      const nestedPrefix = prefix
    37	        ? `${prefix}${NESTED_COMMAND_SEPARATOR}${entry.name}`
    38	        : entry.name
    39	      commands.push(
    40	        ...discoverCommandsFromDir(join(commandsDir, entry.name), scope, nestedPrefix),
    41	      )
    42	      continue
    43	    }
    44	
    45	    if (!isMarkdownFile(entry)) continue
    46	
    47	    const commandPath = join(commandsDir, entry.name)
    48	    const baseCommandName = basename(entry.name, ".md")
    49	    const commandName = prefix
    50	      ? `${prefix}${NESTED_COMMAND_SEPARATOR}${baseCommandName}`
    51	      : baseCommandName
    52	
    53	    try {
    54	      const content = readFileSync(commandPath, "utf-8")
    55	      const { data, body } = parseFrontmatter<CommandFrontmatter>(content)
    56	
    57	      const isOpencodeSource = scope === "opencode" || scope === "opencode-project"
    58	      const metadata: CommandMetadata = {
    59	        name: commandName,
    60	        description: data.description || "",
    61	        argumentHint: data["argument-hint"],
    62	        model: sanitizeModelField(data.model, isOpencodeSource ? "opencode" : "claude-code"),
    63	        agent: data.agent,
    64	        subtask: Boolean(data.subtask),
    65	      }
    66	
    67	      commands.push({
    68	        name: commandName,
    69	        path: commandPath,
    70	        metadata,
    71	        content: body,
    72	        scope,
    73	      })
    74	    } catch {
    75	      continue
    76	    }
    77	  }
    78	
    79	  return commands
    80	}
    81	
    82	function discoverPluginCommands(options?: CommandDiscoveryOptions): CommandInfo[] {
    83	  const pluginDefinitions = discoverPluginCommandDefinitions(options)
    84	
    85	  return Object.entries(pluginDefinitions).map(([name, definition]) => ({
    86	    name,
    87	    metadata: {
    88	      name,
    89	      description: definition.description || "",
    90	      model: definition.model,
    91	      agent: definition.agent,
    92	      subtask: definition.subtask,
    93	    },
    94	    content: definition.template,
    95	    scope: "plugin",
    96	  }))
    97	}
    98	
    99	function deduplicateCommandInfosByName(commands: CommandInfo[]): CommandInfo[] {
   100	  const seen = new Set<string>()
   101	  const deduplicatedCommands: CommandInfo[] = []
   102	
   103	  for (const command of commands) {
   104	    if (seen.has(command.name)) {
   105	      continue
   106	    }
   107	
   108	    seen.add(command.name)
   109	    deduplicatedCommands.push(command)
   110	  }
   111	
   112	  return deduplicatedCommands
   113	}
   114	
   115	export function discoverCommandsSync(
   116	  directory?: string,
   117	  options?: CommandDiscoveryOptions,
   118	): CommandInfo[] {
   119	  const userCommandsDir = join(getClaudeConfigDir(), "commands")
   120	  const projectCommandsDir = join(directory ?? process.cwd(), ".claude", "commands")
   121	  const opencodeGlobalDirs = getOpenCodeCommandDirs({ binary: "opencode" })
   122	  const opencodeProjectDirs = findProjectOpencodeCommandDirs(directory ?? process.cwd())
   123	
   124	  const userCommands = discoverCommandsFromDir(userCommandsDir, "user")
   125	  const opencodeGlobalCommands = opencodeGlobalDirs.flatMap((commandsDir) =>
   126	    discoverCommandsFromDir(commandsDir, "opencode")
   127	  )
   128	  const projectCommands = discoverCommandsFromDir(projectCommandsDir, "project")
   129	  const opencodeProjectCommands = opencodeProjectDirs.flatMap((commandsDir) =>
   130	    discoverCommandsFromDir(commandsDir, "opencode-project"),
   131	  )
   132	  const pluginCommands = discoverPluginCommands(options)
   133	
   134	  const builtinCommandsMap = loadBuiltinCommands()
   135	  const builtinCommands: CommandInfo[] = Object.values(builtinCommandsMap).map((command) => ({
   136	    name: command.name,
   137	    metadata: {
   138	      name: command.name,
   139	      description: command.description || "",
   140	      argumentHint: command.argumentHint,
   141	      model: command.model,
   142	      agent: command.agent,
   143	      subtask: command.subtask,
   144	    },
   145	    content: command.template,
   146	    scope: "builtin",
   147	  }))
   148	
   149	  return deduplicateCommandInfosByName([
   150	    ...projectCommands,
   151	    ...userCommands,
   152	    ...opencodeProjectCommands,
   153	    ...opencodeGlobalCommands,
   154	    ...builtinCommands,
   155	    ...pluginCommands,
   156	  ])
   157	}

tool_call.name: terminal

summary: Create and run temporary skill alias reproduction

thought.text: I reproduced the exact short-name failure; I’ll record findings.

task_list.title: Phase 1: Reword issue and identify requirements
task_list.notes: Problem: names like `systematic-debugging` are shown as valid skill names, but resolver only accepts `superpowers/systematic-debugging` or `/superpowers:systematic-debugging`. The not-found message is `Skill or command "systematic-debugging" not found. Did you mean: superpowers/systematic-debugging, /superpowers:systematic-debugging?`. Fix should keep presentation and executable identifiers consistent, preferably by accepting unambiguous short aliases. Reproduction: invoke/call resolver with short name, observe failure, retry FQN and observe success. Best practices: modify source only, avoid test edits, preserve namespaced resolution, avoid ambiguous alias surprises, use Bun scripts, keep change minimal.

task_list.title: Phase 2: Run repository tests per README/package scripts
task_list.notes: Ran `bun test` per package/AGENTS guidance. Suite currently reports 4628 pass and 98 fail before source changes, largely unrelated broad-suite failures. Dependencies were already installed, no installs performed.

task_list.title: Phase 3: Explore resolver and skill exposure code
task_list.notes: Related files: `src/tools/skill/tools.ts` exact case-insensitive skill match rejects short basename; `src/hooks/auto-slash-command/executor.ts` maps skills to slash commands and exact matches only; `src/features/opencode-skill-loader/skill-template-resolver.ts` and `src/tools/delegate-task/skill-resolver.ts` resolve `load_skills`; `src/features/opencode-skill-loader/async-loader.ts` creates namespaced names for nested skills. Most likely fix is a shared skill-name alias resolver used by skill tool, slash executor, and template resolver.

task_list.title: Phase 4: Build temporary reproduction script
task_list.notes: Created `/tmp/skill-short-name-repro.ts`. It defines `superpowers/systematic-debugging`, verifies the FQN succeeds, and reproduces the short-name failure: `Skill or command "systematic-debugging" not found. Did you mean: superpowers/systematic-debugging?`.

task_list.title: Phase 5: Analyze exact fix location and approach
task_list.notes: Root issue: execution paths compare only full `LoadedSkill.name`, while UI/presentation can expose basename aliases. Need exact match first, then accept an unambiguous final path segment alias for non-namespaced requests. Ambiguous short names should not silently choose a skill.

task_list.title: Phase 6: Implement minimal non-test source changes
task_list.notes: Modify source only, no test logic changes.

task_list.title: Phase 7: Verify reproduction, edge cases, and related tests
task_list.notes: Run temporary script, related tests, typecheck/build as feasible.

task_list.title: Phase 8: Final review against issue and base commit
task_list.notes: Review diff versus base commit 941e265 and ensure requirements are satisfied.

summary: Update plan with reproduction and exploration findings

content.text: Task list has been updated with 8 item(s).

task_list.title: Phase 1: Reword issue and identify requirements
task_list.notes: Problem: names like `systematic-debugging` are shown as valid skill names, but resolver only accepts `superpowers/systematic-debugging` or `/superpowers:systematic-debugging`. The not-found message is `Skill or command "systematic-debugging" not found. Did you mean: superpowers/systematic-debugging, /superpowers:systematic-debugging?`. Fix should keep presentation and executable identifiers consistent, preferably by accepting unambiguous short aliases. Reproduction: invoke/call resolver with short name, observe failure, retry FQN and observe success. Best practices: modify source only, avoid test edits, preserve namespaced resolution, avoid ambiguous alias surprises, use Bun scripts, keep change minimal.

task_list.title: Phase 2: Run repository tests per README/package scripts
task_list.notes: Ran `bun test` per package/AGENTS guidance. Suite currently reports 4628 pass and 98 fail before source changes, largely unrelated broad-suite failures. Dependencies were already installed, no installs performed.

task_list.title: Phase 3: Explore resolver and skill exposure code
task_list.notes: Related files: `src/tools/skill/tools.ts` exact case-insensitive skill match rejects short basename; `src/hooks/auto-slash-command/executor.ts` maps skills to slash commands and exact matches only; `src/features/opencode-skill-loader/skill-template-resolver.ts` and `src/tools/delegate-task/skill-resolver.ts` resolve `load_skills`; `src/features/opencode-skill-loader/async-loader.ts` creates namespaced names for nested skills. Most likely fix is a shared skill-name alias resolver used by skill tool, slash executor, and template resolver.

task_list.title: Phase 4: Build temporary reproduction script
task_list.notes: Created `/tmp/skill-short-name-repro.ts`. It defines `superpowers/systematic-debugging`, verifies the FQN succeeds, and reproduces the short-name failure: `Skill or command "systematic-debugging" not found. Did you mean: superpowers/systematic-debugging?`.

task_list.title: Phase 5: Analyze exact fix location and approach
task_list.notes: Root issue: execution paths compare only full `LoadedSkill.name`, while UI/presentation can expose basename aliases. Need exact match first, then accept an unambiguous final path segment alias for non-namespaced requests. Ambiguous short names should not silently choose a skill.

task_list.title: Phase 6: Implement minimal non-test source changes
task_list.notes: Modify source only, no test logic changes.

task_list.title: Phase 7: Verify reproduction, edge cases, and related tests
task_list.notes: Run temporary script, related tests, typecheck/build as feasible.

task_list.title: Phase 8: Final review against issue and base commit
task_list.notes: Review diff versus base commit 941e265 and ensure requirements are satisfied.

thought.text: I’ll reason through ambiguity handling before making source edits.

action.thought: The safest fix is not to rename discovered skills or duplicate entries, because that could break deduplication and display ordering. Instead, resolution should accept aliases. Exact namespaced matches should always win. If the request has no slash or colon, compare it against the final path segment of namespaced skill names. If exactly one skill matches that short name, resolve it. If more than one matches, return no match so existing error behavior shows available full names rather than silently choosing. This shared helper can be reused by the skill tool, slash command executor, and template resolver used by task load_skills.

thought.text: I’ll update the plan with implementation and verification results.

task_list.title: Phase 1: Reword issue and identify requirements
task_list.notes: Problem: names like `systematic-debugging` are shown as valid skill names, but resolver only accepts `superpowers/systematic-debugging` or `/superpowers:systematic-debugging`. The not-found message is `Skill or command "systematic-debugging" not found. Did you mean: superpowers/systematic-debugging, /superpowers:systematic-debugging?`. Fix should keep presentation and executable identifiers consistent, preferably by accepting unambiguous short aliases. Reproduction: invoke/call resolver with short name, observe failure, retry FQN and observe success. Best practices: modify source only, avoid test edits, preserve namespaced resolution, avoid ambiguous alias surprises, use Bun scripts, keep change minimal.

task_list.title: Phase 2: Run repository tests per README/package scripts
task_list.notes: Ran `bun test` per package/AGENTS guidance. Suite currently reports 4628 pass and 98 fail before source changes, largely unrelated broad-suite failures. Dependencies were already installed, no installs performed.

task_list.title: Phase 3: Explore resolver and skill exposure code
task_list.notes: Related files: `src/tools/skill/tools.ts` exact case-insensitive skill match rejects short basename; `src/hooks/auto-slash-command/executor.ts` maps skills to slash commands and exact matches only; `src/features/opencode-skill-loader/skill-template-resolver.ts` and `src/tools/delegate-task/skill-resolver.ts` resolve `load_skills`; `src/features/opencode-skill-loader/async-loader.ts` creates namespaced names for nested skills. Most likely fix is a shared skill-name alias resolver used by skill tool, slash executor, and template resolver.

task_list.title: Phase 4: Build temporary reproduction script
task_list.notes: Created `/tmp/skill-short-name-repro.ts`. It first reproduced the failure, then after the fix verified tool short-name, FQN, slash namespace, slash executor short-name, slash executor namespace, and ambiguous short-name rejection. Script was removed after verification.

task_list.title: Phase 5: Analyze exact fix location and approach
task_list.notes: Root issue: execution paths compared only full `LoadedSkill.name`, while UI/presentation can expose basename aliases. Fix: exact matches still win; otherwise an unambiguous final path segment can resolve a namespaced skill. Ambiguous aliases intentionally remain unresolved to avoid selecting the wrong skill.

task_list.title: Phase 6: Implement minimal non-test source changes
task_list.notes: Added `skill-name-resolver.ts`; wired it into `skill-template-resolver.ts`, `tools/skill/tools.ts`, and `hooks/auto-slash-command/executor.ts`; exported from the skill-loader barrel. No test files modified.

task_list.title: Phase 7: Verify reproduction, edge cases, and related tests
task_list.notes: Passed reproduction script, `bun run typecheck`, focused Bun tests for skill tool, slash executor/hook, and skill loader, and `bun run build`. Full `bun test` was run before edits and had existing broad-suite failures: 4628 pass, 98 fail.

task_list.title: Phase 8: Final review against issue and base commit
task_list.notes: Compared source diff against base commit 941e265aa15281445ee2953aa435a1c4010214a6. Requirements satisfied: short names for unambiguous namespaced skills now resolve in the skill tool, slash skill executor, and load_skills template resolver while preserving FQN behavior and rejecting ambiguous short aliases.

action.message:
Implemented the minimal non-test source changes for the skill short-name resolver bug.

Summary of changes:
- Added shared resolver helper: `src/features/opencode-skill-loader/skill-name-resolver.ts`
  - Exact skill names still win.
  - Unambiguous short names, e.g. `systematic-debugging`, now resolve to namespaced skills like `superpowers/systematic-debugging`.
  - Slash namespace form like `/superpowers:systematic-debugging` is normalized for skill resolution.
  - Ambiguous short names remain unresolved to avoid silently choosing the wrong skill.
- Updated `src/tools/skill/tools.ts`
  - The `skill` tool now accepts unambiguous short names for namespaced skills.
  - Exact command names still take priority over short-name skill aliases.
- Updated `src/hooks/auto-slash-command/executor.ts`
  - Slash skill execution now resolves short names and colon namespace form after exact command matching.
- Updated `src/features/opencode-skill-loader/skill-template-resolver.ts`
  - `resolveSkillContent*` and `resolveMultipleSkills*` now use the same alias behavior, so delegated `load_skills` resolution is consistent.
- Exported the helper from `src/features/opencode-skill-loader/index.ts`.

Verification performed:
- Reproduced the original failure with a temporary script before the fix.
- After the fix, verified:
  - `superpowers/systematic-debugging` resolves.
  - `systematic-debugging` resolves.
  - `/superpowers:systematic-debugging` resolves through the skill tool.
  - Slash execution resolves `systematic-debugging`.
  - Slash execution resolves `superpowers:systematic-debugging`.
  - Ambiguous short names are rejected.
- Removed the temporary reproduction script afterward.
- Ran focused tests successfully:

summary: Summarize completed skill short-name resolver fix

content.text:
Implemented the minimal non-test source changes for the skill short-name resolver bug.

Summary of changes:
- Added shared resolver helper: `src/features/opencode-skill-loader/skill-name-resolver.ts`
  - Exact skill names still win.
  - Unambiguous short names, e.g. `systematic-debugging`, now resolve to namespaced skills like `superpowers/systematic-debugging`.
  - Slash namespace form like `/superpowers:systematic-debugging` is normalized for skill resolution.
  - Ambiguous short names remain unresolved to avoid silently choosing the wrong skill.
- Updated `src/tools/skill/tools.ts`
  - The `skill` tool now accepts unambiguous short names for namespaced skills.
  - Exact command names still take priority over short-name skill aliases.
- Updated `src/hooks/auto-slash-command/executor.ts`
  - Slash skill execution now resolves short names and colon namespace form after exact command matching.
- Updated `src/features/opencode-skill-loader/skill-template-resolver.ts`
  - `resolveSkillContent*` and `resolveMultipleSkills*` now use the same alias behavior, so delegated `load_skills` resolution is consistent.
- Exported the helper from `src/features/opencode-skill-loader/index.ts`.

Verification performed:
- Reproduced the original failure with a temporary script before the fix.
- After the fix, verified:
  - `superpowers/systematic-debugging` resolves.
  - `systematic-debugging` resolves.
  - `/superpowers:systematic-debugging` resolves through the skill tool.
  - Slash execution resolves `systematic-debugging`.
  - Slash execution resolves `superpowers:systematic-debugging`.
  - Ambiguous short names are rejected.
- Removed the temporary reproduction script afterward.
- Ran focused tests successfully:
  - `bun test src/tools/skill/tools.test.ts src/hooks/auto-slash-command/executor-resolution.test.ts src/hooks/auto-slash-command/index.test.ts src/features/opencode-skill-loader/skill-content.test.ts src/features/opencode-skill-loader/async-loader.test.ts`
  - Result: 103 pass, 0 fail.
- Ran `bun run typecheck`: passed.
- Ran `bun run build`: passed.
- Ran `git diff --check`: passed.

Note:
- Per Phase 2, I also ran the full `bun test` suite before making source changes. It had existing broad-suite failures: 4628 pass, 98 fail. The focused tests related to this change pass after the fix.
- I did not modify any test files. The untracked `junit.xml` and `reports/` entries were already present/left uncommitted in the working tree.
